title: S1/G1: Well-definedness of the O-slice filtration & existence of the slice tower

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Section S1 (no solution.tex yet).** Any solution must first *define* the $\aO$-adapted slice filtration and prove it is an actual filtration, not just name it.

**To prove.** Let $\tau^{\aO}_{\ge n}\subseteq \mathrm{Sp}^G$ be the localizing subcategory generated by the $\aO$-admissible slice cells $G_+\wedge_K S^{m\rho_K}$ (with $m|K|\ge n$ and the relevant $K$-set/norm $\aO$-admissible). Show:
1. $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ is compactly generated and the generating set is a *set* of compact objects, so Bousfield localization applies (small-object argument).
2. The right-orthogonal $\tau^{\aO}_{<n}=(\tau^{\aO}_{\ge n})^{\perp}$ is well-defined and $\mathrm{Map}(\tau^{\aO}_{\ge n},\tau^{\aO}_{<n})=0$.
3. Every $X$ fits in a functorial cofiber sequence $P^{\aO}_{\ge n}X\to X\to P^{\aO}_{<n}X$, giving the $\aO$-slice tower.
4. State whether you use the coarse (cells $\ge n$) or the regular-slice indexing, and keep it consistent.

Without this, the phrase '$\aO$-slice connectivity' in the characterization has no meaning. Reference: Hill, *Equivariant slice filtration: a primer*; HHR 2016 §4.
